contract: If a feature, ecosystem pattern, or security claim is marked proven, it must have automated coverage and release gates must fail when that proof regresses.

entries.contractNote: Modern JS syntax should parse and normalize inside .fs without forcing a rewrite.
entries.docsNote: Covers module syntax, classes, private fields, static blocks, generators, and async generators.

entries.contractNote: Dynamic import and CommonJS-style require should remain usable in rename-first .fs code.
entries.docsNote: Includes mixed ESM/CommonJS usage inside authored .fs modules.

entries.contractNote: Type-only TS syntax should erase cleanly when authored directly in .fs.
entries.docsNote: Covers interfaces, type aliases, generics, namespaces, and declaration shapes.

entries.contractNote: React-style JSX/TSX should compile directly in .fs with normal typed component authoring.
entries.docsNote: Covers typed component props, TSX page modules, and authored .fs TSX.

entries.contractNote: fn, state, and ~ should remain optional language sugar on top of JS/TS-first .fs authoring.
entries.docsNote: Sugar support is maintained without making it the required authoring model.

entries.feature: Import assertion and multiline import shapes
entries.contractNote: Supported import assertion shapes should survive rename-first conversion into .fs.
entries.docsNote: Current proof covers multiline assertion-like import shapes in migration input.

entries.contractNote: Representative page, route, and API module shapes should build cleanly as .fs projects.
entries.docsNote: Covers a small build corpus with pages, dynamic routes, and API modules.

entries.contractNote: Common Node/server request-handler shapes should work directly in .fs.
entries.docsNote: Includes handler-style modules, params access, request mutation, mixed ESM/CJS helpers, and path/env-adjacent server code.

entries.feature: Node middleware chains, error handlers, and env-aware server flow
entries.contractNote: Middleware chains, error bubbling, and environment-aware server modules should remain valid .fs patterns.
entries.docsNote: Covers sequential middleware, error handling, and process.env usage in server code.

entries.contractNote: React-style modules and TSX authoring should work without forcing a framework rewrite.
entries.docsNote: Current proof covers createElement plus react-dom client/server import surfaces.

entries.feature: React hooks, context, and lazy module patterns
entries.contractNote: React-style hook and lazy-module workflows should remain valid authored .fs patterns.
entries.docsNote: Covers useState, createContext/useContext, lazy-loaded module flow, and composed hook/shared-helper patterns.

entries.feature: Next-style pages, links, and route-adjacent modules
entries.contractNote: Next-style module shapes should be accepted as syntax and framework-adjacent source in .fs.
entries.docsNote: Covers page-style TSX modules, next/link and next/navigation imports, plus shared-lib route-adjacent patterns.

entries.contractNote: Vue-style script modules and composable helpers should work as normal .fs source.
entries.docsNote: Current proof covers Vue createApp/h interop, vue-router module usage, typed composable-style modules, and app-adjacent utility helpers.

entries.contractNote: Composable and store-helper patterns should remain valid .fs source, not migration-only compatibility.
entries.docsNote: Covers ref/computed-based composables and store-like helper return shapes.

entries.contractNote: Store-centric ecosystem imports should bundle cleanly from .fs.
entries.docsNote: Current proof covers writable store usage.

entries.contractNote: Preact h/render APIs should remain accessible from .fs modules.
entries.docsNote: Current proof covers core Preact render flow.

entries.contractNote: Signal-style frameworks should import and bundle from .fs without compatibility rewrites.
entries.docsNote: Current proof covers createSignal/createMemo usage.

entries.contractNote: Subpath export resolution should work for .fs imports against modern packages.
entries.docsNote: Covers scoped package subpath exports, export-condition subpaths, and workspace-linked packages.

entries.contractNote: Dual-mode ESM/CJS package exports should resolve correctly from .fs.
entries.docsNote: Covers modern export-condition resolution.

entries.contractNote: CommonJS packages should remain consumable from .fs through compatibility interop.
entries.docsNote: Covers require-driven compatibility and bundling against npm CommonJS packages.

entries.contractNote: Node built-ins should be importable from .fs wherever the runtime target allows them.
entries.docsNote: Current proof covers node:crypto and node:path.

entries.contractNote: Real ecosystem packages should bundle from .fs without relying only on toy fixtures.
entries.docsNote: Current proof uses real installed dependencies instead of synthetic package mocks alone.

entries.contractNote: The core Node runtime path should remain a release-blocking supported target.
entries.docsNote: Covers app routing, API routes, and production adapter serving.

entries.feature: Node, Vercel, and Cloudflare deploy adapters
entries.contractNote: Core deployment adapters are part of the governed compatibility surface.
entries.docsNote: Current proof covers Node/PM2, Vercel, and Cloudflare adapter generation.

entries.feature: Manual deployment to custom Node and container platforms
entries.contractNote: Teams deploying on custom platforms should be able to ship the built dist artifact and start the production runtime without relying on a first-party adapter.
entries.docsNote: Covers the dist-manifest-driven production start path for custom Node/container hosts such as Cloud Run, ECS/EC2, or Oracle compute.

entries.feature: CLI, lint, format, and typecheck stability
entries.contractNote: The core toolchain should remain stable under release gates, not just the parser.
entries.docsNote: Governed through the full repo quality gate.

entries.contractNote: The editor surface should stay aligned with the compatibility contract.
entries.docsNote: Smoke-tested LSP and syntax integration.

entries.contractNote: Public performance and proof claims must stay under automated discipline.
entries.docsNote: Keeps proof pack claims tied to generated artifacts and release gates.

entries.contractNote: Decorator support is not yet a governed proven guarantee.
entries.docsNote: Keep visible as an explicit compatibility lane instead of implying support.

entries.contractNote: Next-style layout and metadata-like exports are part of the governed compatibility surface.
entries.docsNote: Covers metadata-like exports, generated metadata, and layout-style wrappers.

entries.contractNote: A real product-shaped app written in ordinary TypeScript inside .fs should build, start, deploy, and exercise frontend, backend, jobs, and auth together.
entries.docsNote: Covers the Agency Ops proving-ground app: ordinary TypeScript authoring, dashboard routes, session bootstrap, work-item flow, assignment workflow, billing, jobs, and Cloudflare-ready generation.

entries.feature: Authenticated dashboard assignment and workload flows
entries.contractNote: Role-aware dashboard mutations such as assignment, reassignment, workload views, and authenticated operational flows should remain valid .fs product patterns.
entries.docsNote: Covers assignment-capable work-item APIs, seeded assignee state, workload views, and authenticated dashboard mutation flow in Agency Ops.

entries.feature: Authenticated billing reminder and follow-up flows
entries.contractNote: Product-shaped billing flows such as due and overdue invoice visibility, reminder queueing, resend actions, and follow-up history should remain valid .fs dashboard patterns.
entries.docsNote: Covers invoice reminder queue/send actions, due and overdue invoice visibility, reminder history, and billing-to-ops follow-up flow in Agency Ops.

entries.contractNote: New language additions should be incorporated into the compatibility matrix as they stabilize.
entries.docsNote: 4.0 establishes the governed system that future additions plug into.
